entry.title: How to Display a Selective Sales Team Hierarchy in SAP Sales Cloud Reports
entry.content:
In modern businesses, especially large organizations, the hierarchical structure of the sales team is crucial. Managers at various levels are responsible for their subordinates, and for effective management, they need access to data about the entire team structure. However, SAP’s standard reporting capabilities may not be sufficient if reports need to display only certain hierarchy levels instead of the entire management structure. 

Business Case 

A company has a five-level hierarchy (CEO – Region Manager – Channel Manager – Supervisor – Sales Executive). For a sales report, the business requires data grouping and display of product volume in active Leads as follows: Region Manager \ Channel Manager \ Sales Executive. The CEO and Supervisor levels should be excluded. 

Key Challenges 

  • Complexity in Reporting: Standard SAP reports and data sources do not allow selective display of the required hierarchy levels. For example, the business may need to see sales or lead data for mid-level managers (Region Manager, Channel Manager, Sales Executive) but excluding CEO and Supervisor levels. 
  • Limitations of SAP Standard Tools: Standard data sources in SAP either display only the direct manager of an employee or the entire organizational hierarchy, which does not align with business needs. 

In standard SAP reports and data sources, when displaying information about responsible persons, the available options are usually limited to: 

  • The individual (Employee/Sales Executive/Lead Owner). 
  • Their functional manager. 

This does not resolve the need for a selective hierarchy display. As a result, the data remains unstructured, and managers cannot get a clear picture of their teams' work. 

Typically, consultants create a separate combined data source for each report to include the required manager levels. This approach is used repeatedly for every specific report.  

Solution 

To address this challenge, SAP standard tools can be used with some enhancements. Specifically, a new datasource can be created that includes multiple levels of managers at once. This can be achieved using Join Type = Left Outer Join with the "Employee master data" source. 

Steps to Implement: 

  1. Create a new data source with Join Type = Left Outer Join. 
  2. Use "Employee master data" as the base data source. 
  3. Sequentially join additional levels, where the manager from the previous data source becomes the Employee in the next data source. 
  4. Continue this process until the required hierarchy depth is reached. 
  5. Keep in mind that a Left Outer Join can include no more than five data sources in a single join.  

 

As a result, the new data source will include the full list of employees along with all relevant management levels for each employee. This data source can be universally used for various analytical reports, allowing different hierarchy levels to be displayed as needed. 

This is especially useful in cases where the business has not predefined the exact hierarchy level required for reporting. Managers can independently adjust the grouping and data presentation, ensuring access to the necessary organizational structure levels. This approach enhances team visibility, simplifies decision-making, and improves overall management transparency. 

Conclusion 

Properly structured sales team hierarchy reporting is a key factor in effectively managing large teams. By leveraging SAP standard tools with additional enhancements, businesses can significantly improve the quality and usability of reports. This, in turn, enhances managerial efficiency and provides deeper insights into team performance.

entry.title: National ID integration in between Recruitment Management and Onboarding 2.0
entry.content:
Introduction

We would like to share our experiences of using National ID mapping Integration in between Recruitment Management and Onboarding 2.0. It provides solution to complex client requirements to integrated candidate national ID information only for selected countries in between RCM and ONB 2.0. Here, we are presenting you how to integrate national ID information from RCM to Onboarding 2.0 for only selected countries. This will help you address similar requirements in your projects.

In general, National ID information will capture by New Hire in personal data collection step during Onboarding 2.0 process. But in our current project, the client has running operations in 80+ countries, only USA external candidate should capture National ID information in RCM and rest of the country’s external candidates will capture during Personal Data Collection step in Onboarding process.

Trends/Problem Statement / Overview

Scenario 1:  The customer wants to capture new hire national ID information for USA only in Recruitment management and it should be integrated with Onboarding 2.0

Scenario 2: Rest of the countries national ID will capture during Onboarding process (Rest of the countries, new hires will capture national ID during Onboarding process)

Challenges

As per the current SuccessFactors system design, if we do national ID mapping in the recruit to hire data field mapping in Onboarding, the system will have limitations because the national ID mapping will apply to all countries after initiating onboarding from Recruitment and the result is as follows:

  • The onboarding process will only be successful for the country where we capture the national ID in Recruitment.
  • The onboarding process will fail for the remaining countries that did not capture national ID information during recruitment and candidates will appear in Onboarding BPE error tile.

Solution

Scenario 1 (For example USA):

  • We need to create specific job application template for USA and there we need to define the field for SSN. So, external candidate will capture SSN during the recruitment process.
  • Configured Business Rule as shown in below

 

  • Placed business rule in Manage Rules in Recruitment as shown in below

 

  • National ID mapping in recruit to hire data field mapping.

  • Create an SAP incident in SAP launchpad and request SAP to run the script

Please find below the KBA for more information.

https://me.sap.com/notes/3048929

 

Scenario 2 (Global Countries) :

  • We need to create specific application template for global countries without national ID field. So, external candidate will capture national ID information during the Onboarding process.

Expected Output / Outcome

Scenario 1:  As soon as initiated Onboarding from RCM, the onboarding process initiated successfully for USA and can see new hire record in Onboarding dashboard. The responsible group user (who will do review new hire data step) can see SSN information in review new hire data step.

Scenario 2: As soon as initiated Onboarding from RCM, the onboarding process initiated successfully for Global countries and can see new hire record in Onboarding dashboard.

Benefits

The main benefits are as follows:

  • It provides solutions to trigger Onboarding process for only specific countries from Recruitment where The onboarding process will only be successful for the country where we capture the national ID in Recruitment.
  • The rehire process for candidates who have national ID details captured during the recruitment process will be determined by the system and they will be placed in the first rehire check by matching their exact profile.
  • The onboarding process will get success for the remaining countries that did not capture national ID information during recruitment.

Conclusion

As per the current SuccessFactors system design, if we do national ID mapping in the recruit to hire data field mapping in Onboarding, the system will have limitations because the national ID mapping will apply to all countries. This document will help to the customers who need the National ID information for specific countries during the recruitment process. The detailed configuration steps of this document will helpful to trigger Onboarding process for only specific countries from Recruitment where The onboarding process will only be successful for the country where we capture the national ID in Recruitment and simultaneously the onboarding process will get success for the remaining countries that did not capture national ID information during recruitment.

SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding

entry.title: 🇺🇸 Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core (Houston, Texas, USA)
entry.content:
This SAP CodeJam event is on the topic of Getting started with Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core and Python. You do not need to have any background in the topic, but a lot of curiosity!

In this CodeJam you will learn how to use Generative AI Hub on SAP AI Core to implement a retrieval augmented generation (RAG) use case to improve the responses of large language models (LLMs) and reduce hallucinations. You will learn how to deploy an LLM and the orchestration service on SAP AI Core and query it via SAP AI Launchpad and the Python SDK. Furthermore, you will learn about the most important genAI concepts and create and use text chunks and embeddings to improve your RAG response.

Prerequisites

  • Bring your own laptop with power adapter
    • It can be helpful to bring a tablet or second laptop, to look at the instructions as you work
  • A Chromium-based web browser, like Google Chrome, MS Edge, Vivaldi, Brave etc.
  • Check these prerequisites for further information: CodeJam Prerequisites

Location and Date 

This is an in-person event only (not virtual) and is planned for Friday, April 11th, 2025 from 9:00am to 3:00pm local time in Houston, Texas (CDT) 🇺🇸.

Event Address: 6001 Savoy Drive Houston, TX 77036 

  • The event is kindly being hosted by Sierra Digital AppHaus – THANK YOU!
  • The instructors are SAP Developer Advocates @thomas_jung and @MichelleMoudy .

Please note that spaces are limited! If you want to attend, register by clicking the Yes button in the Will you be attending? section above to the right.

For more information on SAP CodeJam events, to ask questions, and to chat with your fellow attendees, head over to the SAP CodeJam group.

entry.title: Re: Setting Up the Latest Benefits Enrollment Experience: Step-by-Step Configuration and Helpful Tip
entry.content:
Hello,

We're ready to begin the transformation to the new benefits UI, but having a difficult time determining when to roll this out to our employees for the best experience possible for both them and our administrators.

Our annual open enrollment is scheduled to take place the first part of November.  Would it be suggested to roll the new UI out before open enrollment so that our employees are able to learn of the new UI and get used to it so that during open enrollment they are only having to learn about changes to the benefits instead of both the new UI and benefit changes?

The other option would be to rollout the new UI at the same time as open enrollment.

Please share best practices, learning experiences, employee and administrator feedback post a rollout.

Thank you,

entry.title: IPPE Master Data Creation Process in - SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi SAPians,

Introduction:

  • Let's have an introduction and overview of the functionality of IPPE (Integrated Product and Process Engineering).
  • This information is primarily utilized for products with several variations. The IPPE model allows the keeping about master data since it includes Process Structure, Product Structure, and Line Design.
  • A entire production process can be mapped using a single model that includes BOM, Routing, and Line design.
  • It is only appropriate for Repetitive manufacturing.

Steps to follow:

Create the Class for the header material, assign the class in Clasification view.

Using the T-Code as PPE to create the IPPE process.

Product Structure:

It describes a product's structure as well as the components used to make it.

Click the create option, Choose the application as product structure, node and class.

Click the create button.

  • In Product Structure, Access node and Structure node will be created.

Access Node:

An access node is a sort of IPPE node that provides access to the product structure and can be used to plan material requirements.

Enter the description, in Access variant tab enter the header material and quantity.

Save the Access node creation.

 Right click the access node go to the way as per below screenshot.

Structure node:

Create the Structure node.

A structure node represents a component or function within a product structure.

Move to the variants tab enter the components (Raw material) and quantity.

If the component has variant move to the object dependencies tab.

Just drag and drop it to the material.

Save the Structure node creation.

Process Structure:

A process structure is a key application object that represents routing data, specifically for repetitive manufacturing.

Click the create icon, choose the application as process structure, type as line routing and enter the class to create it.

Line routing:

It defines the sequence of activities and operations needed to manufacture a product for shop-floor-oriented manufacturing.

Right Click line routing – Create relationship – activity.

In the activity creation move to the modes tab create the resource successfully.

Factory layout:

Factory layout is the systematic representation of assembly lines within a plant. It is a hierarchical structure of assembly line that can have as many levels as needed to represent the factory.

Click the create icon enter the application as factory layout select the type as line and create it.

In basic data view enter the plant and resource which you have already created.

Move to the action points tab enter the start line (1st operation) and end line which refers (last operation).

Move to the line balance tab create the line balance.

Line Balance:

Line balancing is the process of calculating a line's takt time and then altering the line's activities to match the time.

Select the line balance and click the effectivity enter the valid from date.

Save the Line balance creation.

Production Version Creation:

Create the production version, go to the environment and create it.

Fill all data what you have created like product structure, process structure and line activity.

Save the production version.

Here you can be able to see Production Version what you have created.

Right Click the PV go to reports and click the IPPE consistency check.

You have got some warning message in that line balance is yet create.

So, click the icon shown in screenshot – go to environment – select the type as line balance – enter it.

Create the line balance relationship and save it.

Convert the IPPE data into APO data:

The IPPE master data is used to plan requirements using the Planning Matrix in Production Planning and Detailed Scheduling (PP/DS).

Use the transaction code of PPEAPO to convert the IPPE transaction to APO data.

Enter the material, Plant, PV and click the execute.

Resource creation in APO:

Create the resource using the T-code SAPAPORES1.

Enter the resource what you have created and enter the plant.

Choose Change Resource.

Enter the time cont. Capacity

Select the line item, Choose the model assignment which I have highlighted below.

Conclusion:

I hope this is useful for all of our community Team members regarding IPPE master data creation and convert it into as APO (PPDS) data.

I believe, it will be helpful and will provide sufficient information.

Thanks for Reading. Please share your reviews & feedback…

entry.title: SAP Make-to-Order Insights
entry.content:
Today we will learn about the Make to order process used in SAP, the blog will give you an understanding of the business process and the configuration steps.

Strategies which businesses follow to produce goods are as follows:

Make to Order (MTO): The product is not manufactured in advance, Product is produced once the order is received, like Cars, Trucks, Bus’s etc.

Make to Stock (MTS): The product is manufactured in advance, it is produced in mass and fast moving in nature, such as consumer products, pharmaceuticals, household items, etc. 

 

 

Among these two production strategies we will discuss the Make to Order (MTO) business process in this blog.

Make to Order (MTO) Production Strategy:

"Make to order" refers to a production strategy where a company only manufactures a product once a customer places an order, meaning they don't produce goods in advance and stockpile inventory; this approach prioritizes customization and flexibility, allowing for tailored products based on individual customer needs, while minimizing the risk of overproduction and associated inventory costs, but often results in longer lead times for customers as production begins after the order is received.

Example- Cars, Trucks, bus’s etc.

Benefits:

  1. Reduce Inventory cost as production starts after order, saving on storage space of product.
  2. Increase customer satisfaction as they get what they want, Leads to higher satisfaction and loyalty.
  3. Greater flexibility as companies can adapt to changing market demands and quickly introduce new product variations based on customer feedback.
  4. Less waste as no overproduction.

Challenges:

  1. Potential for higher costs as it will be produced for special customers.
  2. Efficient production planning to meet customer deadlines.
  3. Longer lead time as production starts after receiving order. 

When to use?

  1. High production customization, when customer requires unique features or options on their products.
  2. Fluctuating demand: If customer demand is unpredictable and varies significantly, make to order can prevent overstocking. 
  3. High-value products: For expensive items minimizing inventory is crucial. 

Process flow for Make to Order:

Standard Configuration guide to activate MTO:

  1. Item category group: 0001/0002
  2. Item Category: TAK
  3. Schedule line category: CP
  4. MRP Type: ND/PD
  5. Special stock indicator: E
  6. Item category determination: OR + 0001+ Blank + Blank =TAK
  7. Strategy group:020
  8. Requirement Class: 040
  9. Requirement type: KE

Pre-requisites:

  •       In Material master settings that need to be carried out are as follow:
  • In Sales: Sales org. 2 screen enter Item Category group= 0001 (MTO) / 0002 for Configurable material.
  • In MRP1 screen, MRP type= PD/ND (No planning) & MRP controller = 101
  • In MRP3 screen, Strategy Group= 20 (Make to order/No assembly order).
  • In MRP3 screen, Availability check= 01 (Daily requirement).
  • Depending on business requirement you can create or use standard Sales document type, Item category & Schedule line category.

    Process Steps:

    • Create sales order

    Note: Costing for MTO process depends on Sales order, enter EK02 condition manually.

              
    • After creating sales order, we need to check whether the requirement has transferred to MRP using MD04.
    • Requirements planning in (MRP) is a manufacturing process control that includes production planning, scheduling, and stock requirement, enter transaction code: MD02, enter material, plant, MRP control parameters & execute it.
             
    • Convert planned order to Production order using MD04, Choose -> Prod. Order (Convert planned order into production order).
             
    • Release the Production order by clicking on flag and save the Prod. Order.
    • Withdrawing the Material for the Production Order (System creates a reservation for the relevant material components when you make a production order) using MIGO.
    • Confirm the production order using CO11N.
    • Create goods receipt against sales order using MIGO, Enter Movement type as 101.
    • Check the stock overview using MMBE.
    • The schedule lines will be confirmed in sales order, therefore create delivery & post goods issue.
    • Once PGI is done create Billing document.

Expected errors and their prospect solutions are as follow:

  • Error: Sales order does not trigger production order

Prospect solution: Assign correct requirement type to the item category and Ensure requirement class is defined.

  • Error: MRP does not generate planned orders.

Prospect solution: Check strategy group in material master and assign the correct requirement type.

  • Error: Production order does not consume sales order stock.

Prospect solution: Set special stock indicator as” E” in MRP 4 view of material master and ensure MRP settings align with MTO requirements.

  • Error: Sales order confirmation ignores component availability (ATP Not Considering Component Lead Times).

Prospect solution: Check ATP scope in OVZ9 and maintain proper AATP settings and ensure component lead times in material master.

Conclusion: Make-to-Order (MTO) process, stock can be customized, manufacturing can only begin only after receiving a confirmed customer order. Its primary advantages are lower inventory costs, greater customer satisfaction, enhanced production planning, and resource allocation efficiency.

entry.title: Complex Outbound Process: POSC , Wave, WOCR ,Packing Specs , 2-Step Picking vs. Outbound LOSC
entry.content:
This blog simplifies the complex Outbound Delivery Process by providing detailed configuration steps and real-world scenarios.

It also highlights the key differences between 2-Step Picking and Outbound LOSC, helping you better understand and optimize your SAP EWM operations.

                                                        Scenario Assumption:

Let’s Assume an Electronics Product Dealer have Ordered 50 Tablets and 10 Mobile Phones:

✅50 Tablets – Stored on a Mezzanine Floor
✅10 Mobile Phones – Stored on the First Level

  • Since the tablets are stored on the Mezzanine Floor, a Two-Step Picking process is used:
    Bulk Picking – The required 50 pieces of tablets are picked from the mezzanine and placed in an Intermediate Picking Storage Type.
    Sorting & Distribution – The picked stock is placed into a Pick-HU (Handling Unit) and transported to the Packing Center for final processing.
  • Meanwhile, the 10 Mobile Phones stored on the First Level are directly picked using a Pick-HU and moved to the Packing Center.
  • At the Packing Center, mobile phones are packed together according to the Packaging Specification defined in SAP EWM.
  • Once packed, the order follows below process:
    Step 1: Move to the Staging Area.
    Step 2: Prepared shipments are loaded onto the truck.
    Step 3: Goods Issue (GI) is triggered in the system, and an Outbound Delivery is created.

Flow Chart for the Process:

 

                                              Configuration Required:

  • POSC
  • Two-step Picking
  • Pick-HU
  • Picking Strategies
  • Wave
  • WOCR
  • Packaging Specification

                                                           POSC Configuration:

 Spro --> Ref IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define Process-Oriented Storage Control

External Storage Process Step:

Storage process:

Assign Storage Process to External Steps:

POSC:

Define WPT:

Spro --> img --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task

Define WPT:

PTDI:

Determine WPT:

                                              Two-Step Picking:

Define WPT:

Spro --> img --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task

                 *** No PTDI and Determination of WPT is required for Two-step Picking.

Two-Step Picking:

SCM EWM --> EWM --> Goods Issue Process --> Wave Management --> General Settings --> Set-up Two Step Picking.

                                                                   Pick-HU

Assign bins to Activity Area: (/n/scwm/seba)

WPT for Pick HU:

Spro --> img --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Task --> Define WPT

Spro --> img --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Define Warehouse Number Control

                                                   Picking Strategies:

Spro --> IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Goods Issue Process --> Strategies

Define Storage type search Sequence for Stock Removal:

2 Step Picking:

Picking for POSC:

Stock Removal Control Indicator:

Storage Type Search Sequence : Stock Removal

Optimize Access Strategies:

                                                      Wave Management:

Spro--> img --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Goods Issue Process --> Wave Management --> General Settings:

Spro--> img --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Goods Issue Process --> Wave Management --> Wave Template Determination

Assign Procedure to Document Type:

Wave Template Determination (/n/scwm/wavetmp):

Determine Condition Records (T-code : /n/scwm/wdgcm)

                                                                    WOCR:

Spro --> Img --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Cross-Process Settings --> Warehouse Order

Define Packing Profile for Warehouse Order Creation:

Define Search Sequence for Creation Rules for Activity Areas:

                                                  Packaging Specification:

Spro --> IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Packaging Specification :

 Define Number Range for Packaging Specification:

Define Packaging Specification Group:

Spro --> IMG --> SCM EWM --> EWM --> Master Data --> Packaging Specification --> Maintain Structure for Packaging Specification :

Define Level Type:

Define Element Type:

Define Level Set:

Assign the Level Set in Packaging Specification Group:

Define Work Step Type:

Define Packaging Material Types:

Assign Packaging Material Type to Element Type:

Assign Work Step Type to Packaging Material Type:

Maintain Packaging Work Step: (/n/scwm/psworkstep)

Maintain Pack Spec (/n/scwm/Packspec):

                                                                     Testing:

  • Create a Sales Order in VA01 and Outbound Delivery Vl31n and Corresponding to that Sales Order and Distribute them into EWM.

Now Go to the T-code: /n/scwm/PRDO

  • Now Go to the Monitor and see the Deliveries associated with the Wave 5026 and you find the Both Deliveries which are associated with the Wave.

Now Go to Wave in Outbound Side and see the Products which are associated with the Wave:

Now Confirm the Open Task for the Product 6457 and see the Changes:

  • After Confirming the Task, Automatically another Task has been Opened for the Product 6457 and at present it is 2010 Storage Type which is the Picking Area for the Products .( All the Products which are picked in Bulk are now in 2010 Picking Area).
  • Also, for the Product 6457 , Storage Process and also Step is also assigned Automatically after Confirming the Open Task.

Now Confirm the Open Task for the Product 6457 and 6458:

  • Once the task have confirmed for Product 6457 , the Destination bin have changed to Pick-Hu-bin from 8030-Pack-bin which means the product have been picked up by Pick-Hu from the Storage Type 2010.
  • Once the Open Task got Confirmed for Product 6458 , the Destination Bin Changes to Pack-Hu-bin from 8030-Pack-bin which means that Product has been Picked from the Storage Type PG10.
  • Also , the Destination HU number also got Changed for the Product 6458 Once it was picked by Pick-HU-bin because of the Packaging Specification Configuration.

Now check the WOCR which got Triggered for the Both the Deliveries basing on WOCR Configuration:

Now Come back to Deliveries and Process the Delivery Related to 6457 Product.

Task has been Confirmed and Product have been moved to Packing Station as shown Below:

Now go to T-code /n/scwm/pack:

Now go to Monitor and Refresh it:

An Automatic open Task have been Created by the system which shows the 50 Quantities of the product 6457 have been packed in the box and ready to move to Staging area.

Now Confirm the Open Task to move the Product to Staging Area:

Now once when the Task is Confirmed , it open another Task which shows the product 6457 should move to Door or Loading Zone

Once the Loading Zone task have been completed , Warehouse Task’s for the Product 6457 will be completed.

Now move to another Delivery for the Product 6458 :

Now move to the Packing Station /n/scwm/pack:

Confirm the Open Task for Loading Zone and Delivery will be completed.

 Now Go back to Desktop and Click on Refresh and Click on Goods Issue to Generate the Outbound Delivery:

Now check the Outbound Delivery for each Outbound Delivery Order:

Differences between 2-Step Picking and LOSC in Outbound Side:

                                                           Conclusion:

Optimizing outbound delivery in SAP EWM boosts efficiency, accuracy, and order fulfillment. By leveraging Wave Management, WOCR, 2-Step Picking, and Packing Specifications, businesses can streamline operations and reduce errors. Understanding 2-Step Picking vs. LOSC helps in designing an optimal strategy for faster deliveries and better customer satisfaction.

As logistics evolve, adopting SAP EWM best practices is key to enhancing supply chain efficiency. 

Have you implemented these strategies? Share your insights in the comments!"

entry.title: Understanding Warehouse Process Determination and Put away Strategy in SAP EWM
entry.content:
Working in SAP Extended Warehouse Management (EWM) over the years, I’ve seen how warehouse process determination and put away strategies can make or break efficiency in a warehouse. Whether you’re working on an implementation, troubleshooting an issue, or optimizing processes, understanding how SAP EWM assigns Warehouse Process Types (WPTs) and determines storage locations is essential.

In this blog, we’ll break down how warehouse process determination works and how putaway strategies are applied in SAP EWM.

🛠 How SAP EWM Decides Warehouse Process Types (WPTs)

In any warehouse, every inbound, outbound, or internal movement needs a defined process. SAP EWM determines this through a structured set of rules, which I’ve found crucial when configuring and debugging warehouse flows.

🔄 The Flow of Warehouse Process Type Determination

1. Warehouse Number: Defines the physical warehouse where goods are processed.

2. Document Type & Item Type: Dictate whether the task is for an inbound delivery, stock transfer, or outbound order.

3. Delivery Priority: Helps in prioritizing urgent shipments and influencing system behavior.

4. Process Type Determination Indicator (PTDI): A lesser known but powerful setting in the Product Master that drives WPT determination.

5. Warehouse Process Type (WPT): Where everything comes together defining whether the system should create a putaway, picking, or internal movement task.

👉 Real-world lesson: I once worked with a client whose deliveries weren’t triggering the right process. The issue? PTDI settings in the Product Master were missing! Always double-check this configuration when process determination fails.

📍 Putaway Strategy: Where Does the Product Go?

Once SAP EWM determines the WPT, the next step is deciding where to store the product. A well-configured putaway strategy ensures that warehouse space is utilized optimally and picking efficiency is maintained.

📦 How Putaway Works in SAP EWM

🔹 Putaway Control Indicator (PACI): A critical setting in the Product Master that dictates storage preferences.

🔹 Warehouse Process Type (WPT): Ensures that putaway follows the correct strategy (e.g., bulk storage vs. high rack).

🔹 Storage Type Search Sequence (STSS): The system searches for available storage areas in a predefined order (Storage Type 1 → Storage Type 2 → etc.).

👉 Consultant’s Tip: If your products aren’t being stored in the expected bins, start debugging by checking STSS and PACI settings in the Product Master - this is often the root cause!

📋 Let’s Explore a Simple Test Case to Validate the Warehouse Process Flow

📝 Test Case

1. Create a Purchase Order (ME21N)

2. Create Inbound Delivery in S/4 and Distribute It to EWM (VL31N)

3. Maintain Inbound Delivery (/N/SCWM/PRDI)

📌 Delivery Distributed to EWM with Document Number: 180003331

When an Inbound Delivery (INB) is created for a warehouse number configured in Warehouse Process Type Determination, the system automatically assigns Warehouse Process Type 1010. This process type also defines the Goods Movement Bin, pulling its values from the configuration, including the Source Storage Type and Storage Bin. As a result, warehouse tasks are triggered seamlessly, ensuring a smooth inbound process, just as shown in the image.

📦 Goods Receipt and Putaway Execution

Once the inbound delivery is distributed and the shipment arrives, the next steps in SAP EWM are to:

1. Unload the goods 🏗

2. Perform Goods Receipt (GR) ✅

3. Move products to designated storage bins 📍

🚀 Warehouse Task Creation

📌 T-Code: /N/SCWM/PRDI → Inbound Delivery → Follow On Functions → Warehouse Task

🔗 Synchronization Between WPT and Putaway Strategies

For a successful putaway warehouse task, there must be seamless synchronization between Warehouse Process Type Determination and Putaway Strategies.

📌 The Warehouse Process Type and Warehouse Number act as the triggers for putaway execution.

📌 The final bin or storage type is determined through the STSS configured in the system. 📌 For multiple products across different storage types, the PACI plays a critical role in directing products to the right storage bins, ensuring an optimized and efficient putaway process.

🏁 Conclusion

A well-structured warehouse process starts with the right Warehouse Process Type (WPT) trigger, ensuring that every inbound movement follows a defined workflow. In SAP EWM, the Warehouse Number and Document Type act as key triggers, assigning the correct WPT, which in turn defines the Goods Movement Bin, source storage type, and putaway method. This process is crucial for seamless warehouse task execution and avoiding inefficiencies.

Once the warehouse process type is determined, the system moves to the putaway strategy, ensuring that products are stored in the right locations based on Storage Type Search Sequence (STSS) and Putaway Control Indicator (PACI). These settings guide the system in allocating storage bins, optimizing warehouse space, and maintaining efficient picking and retrieval operations.

For a successful putaway process, synchronization between Warehouse Process Type Determination and Putaway Strategies is essential. When properly configured, EWM automates inbound logistics, enhances inventory accuracy, and minimizes manual intervention, leading to a streamlined and highly efficient warehouse operation.

entry.title: How can you make a button accessible only for a specific login ID in a custom Z report in SAP ABAP?
entry.content:
First, go to System → Status and find the program name of the custom transaction code (T-Code).

Go to the code and press Ctrl + F to search for REUSE_ALV_GRID_DISPLAY. Check how many times it is being called. For each occurrence, find the PF-STATUS parameter. If it's commented out, uncomment it and declare a PF-STATUS for each instance.

 

THEN, we will create a table and declare a field using a Data Element, where only the entries maintained in the TMG will determine access to a specific button.

Go to SE41 and copy the standard PF status for two cases:

  1. One where the button is present.
  2. One where the button is not present.

THEN, develop the code using a PERFORM routine, ensuring that the button visibility is dynamically controlled based on the user command’s ID. The button should be visible only if the user command originates from a specific user ID; otherwise, it should remain hidden.

 

Now, only the user with the assigned access can see the button. For all other users, the button will remain hidden

entry.title: Building an Attachment Upload and Download Solution Using UploadSetwithTable Control
entry.content:
Introduction

The sap.m.plugins.UploadSetwithTable control is the successor of the sap.m.upload.UploadSet control.

It provides a table-based user interface to upload, display, and download files, making it perfect for document management scenarios.

By default, the UploadSetwithTable control performs a POST request to upload attachments.

However, in certain scenarios, the upload process requires a two-step approach:

  1. A POST request to create the attachment record.
  2. A PUT request to upload the actual file content to the record created in the previous step.

This two-step process is required when working with CAP (Cloud Application Programming Model), which is the focus of this blog post. (Note: This approach may differ when using an ABAP backend).

The Problem and the Motivation for the Blog Post

This blog post was written during our Proof of Concept (POC), where we have a requirement to create a reusable attachment component. After some research, I couldn’t find any blog post or example demonstrating how to use the UploadSetwithTable control with a real backend. All available examples, including those on the SAPUI5 documentation site, use a mock server and do not provide a real backend integration.

Thus, I decided to document this solution and share it with the community, as this could potentially save a lot of time for developers facing similar challenges.

Who Is This Blog Post For?

This solution is intended for scenarios where you have a non-draft enabled application or a freestyle application. If you are working with a Fiori application that supports draft functionality, this blog post may not be relevant for you. In draft-enabled applications, you get the complete attachment handling functionality out of the box by implementing the standard CAP attachment plugin.

We initially referred to the following blog post, which provides a good starting point for working with draft-enabled applications.

Implementation Breakdown

1- Backend Design

For our backend design, we utilize the ManagedAttachment entity from cap-js/attachments. This is the same entity used in the central attachment plugin that comes with CAP. We define the following in our CDS model:

 

using {Attachments as ManagedAttachment} from '@cap-js/attachments';

entity Attachments : ManagedAttachment {
ParentId : UUID;
}

 

To add the Attachment plugin, execute the following command 

 

npm add @cap-js/attachments

 

ParentId references the parent record to which the attachment belongs. For example the header or item entity in your list report application.

The ManagedAttachment entity comes with essential properties like filename, mimeType, and content, which are used to manage the attachment's data.

2- XML Cod

 

<core:FragmentDefinition
    xmlns:core="sap.ui.core"
    xmlns="sap.m"
    xmlns:upload="sap.m.upload">
    <Table
        id="attachmentsTable"
        core:require="{handler: 'namespace of your JS handler/controller'}"
        items="{path: 'Attachments', templateShareable: false}"
        mode="MultiSelect"
        selectionChange="handler.onSelectionChange">
        <headerToolbar>
            <OverflowToolbar>
                <ToolbarSpacer />
                <upload.ActionsPlaceholder id="uploadButton" placeholderFor="UploadButtonPlaceholder" />
                <Button
                    id="downloadSelectedAttachments"
                    text="Download"
                    enabled="false"
                    press="handler.onDownloadFiles"
                />
                <Button
                    id="deleteSelectedAttachments"
                    text="Delete"
                    enabled="false"
                    press="handler.onDeleteFiles"
                />
            </OverflowToolbar>
        </headerToolbar>
        <dependents>
            <plugins.UploadSetwithTable
                beforeUploadStarts="handler.onBeforeUploadStart"
                beforeInitiatingItemUpload="handler.onBeforeUploadInit"
                actions="uploadButton">
                <rowConfiguration>
                    <upload.UploadItemConfiguration
                        fileNamePath="filename"
                        urlPath="url"
                        mediaTypePath="mimeType"/>
                </rowConfiguration>
                <uploader>
                    <upload.UploaderTableItem httpRequestMethod="Put"/>
                </uploader>
            </plugins.UploadSetwithTable>
        </dependents>
        <columns>
            <Column id="fileName" importance="High">
                <header>
                    <Label text="Name/Type" />
                </header>
            </Column>
            <Column id="url" importance="High" visible="false">
                <header>
                    <Label text="Url" />
                </header>
            </Column>
        </columns>
        <items>
            <ColumnListItem>
                <HBox>
                    <Link
                        text="{filename}"
                        class="sapUiTinyMarginBottom"/>
                </HBox>
                <Text text="{url}"/>

            </ColumnListItem>
        </items>
    </Table>
</core:FragmentDefinition>

 

It’s important to highlight that we are defining our uploader under the aggregation uploader. This means we will skip the default implementation. if you removed the uploader aggregation, it means you will use the default uploader from the control which might still work for you.

3- JS Code

Before the Upload Start

  • Creating a New Attachment: A new record is created in the backend with a Post request. Note that in the response to the POST request, the content property is still null.
  • Uploading the Content: Once the new attachment record is created and the ID is retrieved, a Put request is sent to upload the file content to the created record.
  • Update the url property: To enable the download functionality using the UploadSetwithTable control, the context of the selected attachment must have the url property filled.

    The url should point to the content property of the created attachment record. This is a crucial step that is not clearly mentioned in the SAPUI5 documentation 🙂.

 

onBeforeUploadStart: function (oEvent) {
    const oTable = oEvent.getSource().getParent();
    const oModel = oTable.getModel();
    const oItem = oEvent.getParameter("item");
    const oBinding = oTable.getBinding("items");
    const oFile = oItem.getFileObject();
    const sServiceUrl = 'your service url as in the manifest file';
    oBinding.create({
        "filename": oFile.name,
        "mimeType": oFile.type
    });
    // Trigger the POst request
    oModel.submitBatch("attachmentsGroup");
    oBinding.attachEventOnce("createCompleted", function (oEvent) {
        const oContext = oEvent.getParameter("context");
        const oUploadSetwithTable = oTable.getDependents()[0];
        const oUploader = oUploadSetwithTable.getUploader(); // Retrieve our uploader defined in the XML
        const sUploadUrlUrl = sServiceUrl + oContext.getPath() + '/content';
        oUploader.setUploadUrl(sUploadUrlUrl);
        oUploader.uploadItem(oItem); // Trigger the Put request
        MessageToast.show("Attachments Added");
        // Update the URL property so it points to the content property
        oContext.setProperty("url", sUploadUrlUrl).then(function () {
            oTable.getBindingContext().requestSideEffects([{ $NavigationPropertyPath: "Attachments" }]);
        });
    });
},

 

Example of how the sUploadUrl looks like in our scenario:

<SERVICE_URL>/<ENTITY>(<PARENT_ID>)/Attachments(<ATTACHMENT_ID>)/content

 

Before The Upload Init

It is important to clear the upload URL from the previous action before initiating a new upload If not, the new content which you are uploading will be pushed to the previous file that you have already attached.

 

        onBeforeUploadInit: function (oEvent) {
            const oUploader = oEvent.getSource().getUploader();
            oUploader.setUploadUrl("");
        }

 

Now we have covered the whole upload logic. The rest is to handle the selection change, download, and delete actions.

 

        onSelectionChange: function(oEvent) {
            const oTable = oEvent.getSource();
            const aSelectedItems = oTable?.getSelectedContexts();
            const oDownloadBtn = this.byId("downloadSelectedAttachments");
            const oDeleteBtn = this.byId("deleteSelectedAttachments");
            if (aSelectedItems.length > 0) {
                oDownloadBtn.setEnabled(true);
                oDeleteBtn.setEnabled(true);
            } else {
                oDownloadBtn.setEnabled(false);
                oDeleteBtn.setEnabled(false);
            }
        },
        onDownloadFiles: function(oEvent) {
            const oTable = this.byId("attachmentsTable");
            const oUploadSet = oTable.getDependents()[0];
            const aContexts = oTable.getSelectedContexts();
            aContexts.forEach(oContext => {
                oUploadSet.download(oContext, true);
            });
        },
        onDeleteFiles: function () {
            const oTable = this.byId("attachmentsTable");
            const aContexts = oTable.getSelectedContexts();
            aContexts.forEach(oContext => {
                oContext.delete().then(()=> {
                    MessageToast.show("Attachments Deleted");
                });
            });
        }

 

Below is a simplified view of the attachments table.

Issue We Faced with the Control:

Even though we are using a custom uploader, which should ideally suppress the default upload behavior of the control, the control still attempts to execute the upload, leading to a UI dump. We plan to discuss this with our CoE, and if it turns out to be a bug in the control, we will work on addressing it accordingly.

I hope this blog post helps the community understand how to integrate the UploadSetwithTable control with real backend systems.

All the best

entry.title: SAP IBP Configuration - Create and activate a Planning Area in SAP IBP
entry.content:
In SAP Integrated Business Planning (IBP), a planning area is a fundamental configuration element that defines the structure and scope of the planning process. It serves as the backbone for planning and analytics activities within SAP IBP. Here are some key components of a planning area:

  • Time Profile: Defines the time dimensions, such as days, weeks, months, and years, used in planning.
  • Master Data Types: Includes data like products, locations, customers, and resources, along with their attributes.
  • Key Figures: Metrics used in planning, such as demand forecasts, sales plans, and supply plans.
  • Planning Levels: Determines the granularity of planning, for example, by product, location, or customer.

The planning area integrates these elements to create a cohesive model for supply chain planning and optimization.

Create a Planning Area

1. On the SAP Fiori launchpad, in the Model Configuration group, choose the Planning Areas Model Configuration tile.

2. The Planning Area screen appears and choose New to create a planning area.

3. Enter ID, Description and time profile in planning area page. (For time profile creation, please reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Time Profile in SAP IBP https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-ibp-configuration-create-a-time-profile-in-sap-ibp/ba-p/13998774)

Enable Supply Planning

If a planning area is enabled for supply planning, the users can invoke advanced supply planning functions, such as heuristics and optimizers.

Enable Order Key Figures

If the planning area is enabled for order key figures, you can use data sources to define master data types and key figures. You can also set the planning area up for order-based planning.

Enable Change History

You can view the change history in the Change History Analysis app and the IBP Excel Add-In.

If you activate this switch and then later deactivate it again, any recorded change history of the planning area is deleted upon the next activation of the planning area.
 

Planning Horizons

The values specify the number of periods in the past and the future for which you can run calculations. The time periods are relative to the present date. You can use the preselected values based on the Default Display Horizon settings for your time profile level or specify a broader horizon.

4. Choose Save to save planning area.

5. Attributes assignment. After planning area saved, go to Attributes tab, add attributes to planning area.

6. Select the checkboxes to the left of master data types, and then choose Add. Selecting the Master Data Type checkbox selects all of the attributes of the master data type.(For master data type creation, please reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Simple Master Data Type in SAP IBP ).

7. Create key figures in planning area. Refenrence SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Key Figure in SAP IBP and SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Key Figure Using an Attribute in SAP IBP

8. Create Version in planning area. Reference SAP IBP Configuration - Create a Version in SAP IBP

 

Activate a Planning Area

1. Check the consistency of time profile.

a. In the SAP Fiori launchpad, in the Model Configuration screen area, choose the Time Profile tile.

b. Select your time profile in the list and choose Check.

c. Check the log by either selecting Application Log on the top right of the screen or the Check-Success hyperlink in the Last Action column for your time profile.

2. Check the consistency of master data types.

a. In the SAP Fiori launchpad, in the Model Configuration screen area, choose the Master Data Types tile.

b. Select your master data types in the list on the left and choose Check → Check Without Dependencies.

c. Check the log by either selecting Application Log on the top right of the screen or the Check: Success hyperlink.

3. Check the consistency of your planning area.

a. In the SAP Fiori launchpad, in the Model Configuration screen area, choose the Planning Areas Model Configuration tile.

b. Select your planning area from the list and choose the Check → With Dependencies option.

c. Check the log by selecting the hyperlink in the Last Action Status column.

4. Activate your planning area.

a. In the SAP Fiori launchpad, on the Model Configuration tab, choose Planning Areas Model Configuration.

b. Select the planning area that you created in the list on the left of the screen.

c. Choose Activate → Full Scope, With Dependencies.

d. In the Planning Area screen, to open the Logs, select the hyperlink in the Last Action Status column.

e. Planning area activated and the status of planning area becomes Active.

entry.title: Integrating Netweaver ≤7.4 Systems with SAP Forms Service by Adobe on Cloud Foundry
entry.content:
Problem Statement

If you are planning to operate your Netweaver ≤7.4 systems with SAP Forms Service by Adobe until the end of the Netweaver's Extended Maintenance in 2030, you might have encountered the problem that these Netweaver releases are only compatible with the SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP Neo, which will be sunset end of 2028. To make things worse, SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP Cloud Foundry is only compatible with Netweaver 7.5 and S/4HANA Foundation systems.

Does that mean that you have to upgrade to Netweaver 7.5 to use SAP Forms Service by Adobe after 2028?

Before 2028, does that mean that you have to operate two instances of SAP Forms Service by Adobe in parallel (one on BTP Neo, one on BTP Cloud Foundry), if you have both Netweaver ≤7.4 systems and Netweaver 7.5 or S/4HANA Foundation systems in your landscape?

Fortunately, the incompatibility is only caused by the authentication mechanism. In this blog, we will cover the steps to integrate Netweaver ≤7.4 system with SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP Cloud Foundry by means of a middleware (e.g. SAP Integration Suite, SAP PI, or any other) that supports OAuth 2.0 authentication. This way, you will only need to operate SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP Cloud Foundry and you avoid an upgrade to Netweaver 7.5, if all you want is to run out the clock until your S/4 Transformation is finished.

 

 

Solution Scenarios & Benefit

This guide is hence particularly useful for customers in these scenarios:

  • No plans to upgrade to ECC 6.0 EhP 8 (i.e. NW 7.5)
  • Plans to operate on ECC 6.0 EhP 7 (i.e. NW 7.4) past 2028 towards 2030
  • Hybrid landscape with legacy ECC and S/4 workloads requiring PDF generation

The benefits of this custom approach include:

  • A unified setup that supports both legacy ECC EhP≤7/NW≤7.4 and new S/4 workloads
  • Reduced infrastructure and support costs
  • Elimination of the need to manually apply security patches

 

 

Feature Comparison of SAP Forms Service by Adobe:
BTP Neo vs. BTP Cloud Foundry

Before we commence with the configuration steps, let's compare the features of Forms Service on Neo and CloudFoundry environments.


 SAP Forms Service by Adobe
on BTP Neo		SAP Forms Service by Adobe
on BTP Cloud Foundry
CompatibilityABAP Netweaver 7.0 SP13 up to Netweaver 7.4

S/4 HANA version 1809 (SP8+), 1909 (SP6+), 2020 (SP4+), 2021, or higher.

Netweaver version 7.50 SP24 or higher

AuthenticationBasic AuthenticationOAuth 2.0
Cloud InfrastructureEnd of life by 2028Future-proof

 

 

Architecture & Solution Concept

Since the incompatibility between Netweaver ≤7.4 and SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP Cloud Foundry is caused by the authentication method (at an identical payload), compatibility can be reestablished by introduction of a middleware that employs BasicAuth towards the Netweaver ≤7.4 system and OAuth 2.0 towards SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP Cloud Foundry while forwarding the payload in both directions.

 

BTP_Forms_Service

 

 

Configuration

1. Middleware
(Integration Suite)

  • Configure the "HTTPS" sender adapter which acts as a proxy to Forms service on Cloud Foundry.
  • Value configured in "Address" field should be used as "Path Prefix" in ADS RFC destination which will be configured later in ABAP system.

Sender_Adapter

  • Configure the "HTTP" receiver adapter to forward the request to Forms service on Cloud Foundry. This adapter will use OAuth 2.0 authentication as required for Forms Service.
    • Generate the service key for Forms service instance before configuring this step.
  • Fill in the following information:
    • Address: uri from service key/AdobeDocumentServicesSec/Config
    • Query: style=rpc
    • Proxy Type: Internet
    • Method: POST
    • Authentication: OAuth2 Client Credentials
    • Credential Name: <Name of secret configured in Integration Suite>

Receiver_Adapter

  • Deploy the artifact.

Full_Integration

 

 

2. Netweaver

  • Update "ADS" RFC in SM59.
    • Host: <Hostname of the middleware>
    • Port: 443
    • Path Prefix: <address configured in Sender adapter>

SM59_1

  • In "Logon & Security" tab update the authentication details.
    • Select "Basic Authentication"
    • User: <provide the Client ID from Service Key generated in Integration suite>
    • Password: <provide the Client secret from Service Key generated in Integration suite>
    • SSL: set to "Active"
    • SSL certificate: select the "SSL Client Anonymous" PSE
      • Before using the PSE, Open https://www.digicert.com/digicert-root-certificates.htm in your web browser and download the following certificates:
        • DigiCert Global Root CA
        • DigiCert Global Root G2
        • ISRG Root X1 and ISRG Root X2 (available for download from Chains of Trust - Let's Encrypt).
      • Use transaction STRUSTSSO2 to import the downloaded certificates to SSL Client (Anonymous) PSE.

SM59_2

  • Save the RFC connection changes
  • Connection test will always end in "500 - Internal Server error" which is expected.

SM59_3

 

 

3. Cloud Connector

  • Open Cloud Connector Administration UI and configure new system mapping (Cloud to On-premise). For details, refer to Configure the Destination in the Cloud Connector | SAP Help Portal.
  • Connect the Cloud Connector with the "Forms Service" sub account provisioned on BTP.
  • After the system mapping is configured, select the row and Choose Add (+) in the Resources Accessible On... section.
    • Add /sap/bc/fp/ (subpaths included) and
    • add /sap/bc/fpads/ (subpaths included).

Connector_1

 

 

4. SAP Forms Service by Adobe

  • Login to BTP Forms service subaccount and configure the destination to ABAP system, destination name should follow this naming convention FP_ICF_DATA_<ABAP SID>.
    • Type: HTTP
    • URL: http://<virtual host name configured in cloud connector>:<virtual port configured in cloud connector>
    • Proxy Type: OnPremise
    • Authentication: BasicAuthentication
    • Location ID(Optional): Enter location ID if configured in Cloud Connector
    • User: System user in ABAP system (Ensure the user has proper authorization. Refer note 3406470)
    • Password: Password of System user
  • In Additional properties section, add New Property
    • sap-client: Client number of ABAP system

BTP_Forms

  • Save and test the connection.

BTP_ConnectionTest

 

 

5. Test PDF generation

You can generate test PDFs using standard report FP_TEST_00 available in ABAP system.

FP_TEST_00 report SE38

This should successfully conclude the configuration enabling your Netweaver ≤7.4-based system to utilize SAP Forms Service by Adobe on BTP Cloud Foundry.

entry.title: Case Expressions in CDS Queries
entry.content:
Case Expressions in CDS Queries: A Practical Guide

Introduction

Core Data Services (CDS) in SAP provide a powerful way to model and retrieve data efficiently. One of the essential features of CDS queries is the CASE expression, which allows for conditional logic within queries. Using CASE expressions can help simplify logic, avoid multiple joins, and improve query performance. In this guide, we will explore how CASE expressions work in CDS views with practical examples.

What is a CASE Expression?

A CASE expression in CDS is used to implement conditional logic within a SELECT statement. It allows you to return different values based on conditions, similar to an IF-ELSE statement in programming.

Syntax of CASE Expression:

 

CASE WHEN <condition> THEN <result>
     WHEN <condition> THEN <result>
     ELSE <default_result>
END

 

The CASE expression evaluates conditions sequentially and returns the first matching result. If no conditions are met, the ELSE clause value is returned.

Use Cases for CASE Expressions in CDS

  1. Mapping Codes to Descriptions
  2. Applying Business Rules Dynamically
  3. Avoiding Joins with Lookup Tables
  4. Data Classification Based on Thresholds

Let's explore these with examples.

Example 1: Mapping Codes to Descriptions

Consider a scenario where we store an order status code but want to display a meaningful status description in the output.

 

DEFINE VIEW ZORDER_STATUS_VIEW AS SELECT FROM zsales_orders
{
    order_id,
    customer_id,
    CASE order_status
         WHEN 'P' THEN 'Pending'
         WHEN 'C' THEN 'Completed'
         WHEN 'X' THEN 'Cancelled'
         ELSE 'Unknown'
    END AS order_status_desc
}

 

Explanation:

  • If order_status is 'P', the result will be 'Pending'.
  • If order_status is 'C', it returns 'Completed'.
  • If order_status is 'X', it returns 'Cancelled'.
  • If none of these conditions match, it defaults to 'Unknown'.

Example 2: Applying Business Rules Dynamically

You can use CASE expressions to apply business rules dynamically, such as categorizing sales values.

 

DEFINE VIEW ZSALES_CATEGORY AS SELECT FROM zsales_data
{
    sales_id,
    sales_amount,
    CASE
        WHEN sales_amount > 10000 THEN 'High Value'
        WHEN sales_amount BETWEEN 5000 AND 10000 THEN 'Medium Value'
        ELSE 'Low Value'
    END AS sales_category
}

 

Explanation:

  • If sales_amount is greater than 10,000, the sale is categorized as 'High Value'.
  • If it's between 5,000 and 10,000, it's 'Medium Value'.
  • Otherwise, it's categorized as 'Low Value'.

Example 3: Avoiding Joins with Lookup Tables

Instead of performing multiple joins to derive values from a lookup table, CASE expressions can simplify the logic directly in the SELECT statement.

 

DEFINE VIEW ZPAYMENT_METHOD_VIEW AS SELECT FROM zpayments
{
    payment_id,
    amount,
    CASE payment_type
        WHEN 'CC' THEN 'Credit Card'
        WHEN 'DC' THEN 'Debit Card'
        WHEN 'PP' THEN 'PayPal'
        ELSE 'Other'
    END AS payment_type_desc
}

 

Benefits:

  • Reduces the need for additional joins.
  • Enhances query readability and performance.

Example 4: Data Classification Based on Thresholds

If you need to classify data dynamically, such as employee performance, CASE expressions can help.

 

DEFINE VIEW ZEMPLOYEE_PERFORMANCE AS SELECT FROM zemployee_data
{
    emp_id,
    emp_name,
    performance_score,
    CASE
        WHEN performance_score >= 90 THEN 'Outstanding'
        WHEN performance_score BETWEEN 75 AND 89 THEN 'Good'
        WHEN performance_score BETWEEN 50 AND 74 THEN 'Average'
        ELSE 'Needs Improvement'
    END AS performance_category
}

 

Best Practices for Using CASE Expressions in CDS

  1. Keep it simple – Avoid excessive nesting of CASE expressions.
  2. Use ELSE clause – Always provide a default value in ELSE to prevent NULL values.
  3. Optimize performance – If CASE expressions become complex, consider moving logic to the application layer.
  4. Test thoroughly – Ensure conditions cover all possible values to prevent unexpected results.

Conclusion

CASE expressions in CDS queries provide a powerful mechanism to apply conditional logic efficiently. By leveraging them, developers can simplify data transformations, enhance performance, and reduce unnecessary joins. Understanding and implementing CASE expressions properly will help in building more efficient SAP applications.

entry.title: Generation of Message Processing logs report for EDI transactions using SAP CI.
entry.content:
Here the requirement to generate the report on ad-hoc basis to check the Orders, Order Acknowledgements and Invoices status based upon the trading partner number in EDI integration. In day-to-day basis customers use to send 1000's of orders there is possibility of missing track of them. in spite of checking using message monitoring page by providing order numbers or trading partner numbers etc., I came across with a solution of using standard API provided by SAP by making some custom changes in the I-Flows of EDI and creating a separate flow for generating a report. Here are the below steps to follow to achieve this.

Firstly, add the custom header to I-Flow’s with your wish and add the Exchange Property as a X-Path and provide the x-path source value in the tab and provide the data type accordingly., here I am using trading partner number as the custom header for all EDI flow's.

Please check the below script for storing custom header

//

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message;

//Add file name to custom header

def Message addFileName(Message message) {

    def messageLog = messageLogFactory.getMessageLog(message);

    if(messageLog != null){

        //Read FileName

                             def fileName = message.getProperty("EDI_TradingPartner");

                    messageLog.addCustomHeaderProperty("EDI_TradingPartner", fileName);

    }

    return message;

}

//

After adding these script and exchange property check that is getting reflected in message monitoring page as below.

We are good w.r.t integration flow design. Now we are good to start with creating an I-Flow for generating report for making our life easier.

Prior to creating the I-flow check the prerequisites.  To consume the standard API to get the message processing logs in SAP CPI, Create an Instance of Process Integration Runtime with Plan API in BTP cockpit of XXX subaccount and store the service key details for future reference. This will be used to extract the .edmx file of ODATA API.

Once the instance is created, please feel to store those credentials in security material and can be used in communication channel.

Create an Integration flow with the name as you wish, once I-flow is created is Please follow below steps.

  1. Create sender channel with https adapter and expose the endpoint by deploying the flow, this URL we will use for sending the request.

     

     Take a content modifier and create an Exchange Property with I-Flow Name Start date, configure source values and externalize them as we are going to change frequently in future.

  2. Now we have standard API in SAP Business Accelerator Hub with below details to extract all the values which is present in the message processing logs like MessageGuid, Correlation, ApplicationMessageId, etc., with below to get these details kindly download OData EDMX from API specification tab and add it to references in your flow as schema.

    Adding address for your reference: https://api.sap.com/api/MessageProcessingLogs/overview
  3.  Take the Request Reply pallet and connect to receiver with OData adapter and maintain below details in the connection parameters and processing steps.Address: https://{your CI tenanturldetails} /api/v1

    Proxy Type as internet and maintain the authentication and provide the authentication details which you have stored in security material.

  4. Now maintain the processing steps as below
    Operation Details : Query(GET)
    Resource Path : MessageProcessingLogs
    Query options : based up on your requirement by using preview steps.

     

  5. Once done with all these, store the required details in exchange property like Status, ApplicationMessageId, IntegrationFlowName and MessageGuid in a content modifier. Here MessageGuid plays a key role in getting the details of custom headers, this API does not provide the details of custom headers in response, to achieve this take one more request reply and send it to receiver via HTTP adapter. Since we have one more standard API for pulling custom headers as listed below.

     

     

  6. Now provide the necessary details in HTTP adapter like address and credentials as below reference.

     

  7. Now, take a content modifier and maintain the Custom Header Name and value in the exchange property to store incoming values and store all the properties in the message body as expression.

     

  8. Now we are almost there, take a gather step to gather all the information with below aggregation strategy.

     

  9. Now, we are in final step take an XML to CSV converter to see the report in CSV format with below configuration.

     

  10. We have completed all the steps in creating a flow and we are good to test and check for the outcome from Postman.

     

    Thank you everyone, I think this will be quite useful for all integration folks who are working with EDI transactions can make their job easy.

    Cheers and happy learning !!

entry.title: Re: WebEx VLS integration with SF LMS for 1H 2023 release
entry.content: Hi there. adding to an old thread because I'm now trying to find out how to integrate using the REST API instead of XML. I can see that the only existing VLS established in our SuccessFactors LMS is using the XML API. I can also see that all of the alleged VLS items are actually in fact ILT or WBT items, not really VLS. I have read the same documentation as well as within Cisco DevNet and looking to determine if anyone internally has used the webex developer tool or the current XML integration, which according to everything I read, is no longer supported by Cisco Webex. Any advice would be truly appreciated.

entry.title: Re: BADI SD_SLS_MODIFY_ITEM not working as expeted
entry.content: I think the BAdI is just working, if something in the position is changed e.g. the quantity.
